relevant_files.role: Core pattern engine with line-by-line regex scanning
SecurityScanner orchestrating diff and file scanning
Data structures for findings and pattern definitions
14 embedded regex patterns for vulnerability detection
Test fixture documenting multi-line vulnerability limitation

conventions.commits: Conventional commits (feat:, fix:, etc.) with GPG signing and DCO sign-off required
conventions.testing: Unit tests in same file with #[cfg(test)] mod tests; integration tests in tests/ directory; AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert)
conventions.linting: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings; workspace lints configured in Cargo.toml with pedantic warnings
conventions.error_handling: thiserror for libraries (used in aptu-core), anyhow for applications; context() for error chaining

patterns: LazyLock static initialization for singleton pattern engine (PATTERN_ENGINE)
Line-by-line iteration with enumerate() for 1-indexed line numbers
File extension filtering in pattern matching logic
Regex::find() for single match per line
Diff parsing with unified format handling (@@, +, - prefixes)

related_issues.title: test: document multi-line vulnerability detection limitation
related_issues.relevance: Closed issue documenting that regex-based scanner cannot detect vulnerabilities split across lines (source and sink on different lines)

related_issues.title: feat(security): evaluate tree-sitter for AST-based vulnerability detection
related_issues.relevance: Target issue proposing tree-sitter evaluation for multi-line pattern support; spike estimate 500-800 LOC; convert 14 patterns to tree-sitter queries

related_issues.title: test: add test_multi_line_vulnerability_not_detected
related_issues.relevance: PR adding test case documenting the limitation; merged before issue #737 opened

constraints: Line-by-line regex scanning prevents detection of multi-line vulnerabilities
PatternDefinition.pattern field is regex string; no AST support
Finding.line_number is single usize; no range support for multi-line matches
Existing 14 patterns are regex-based; conversion required for any new engine
SecurityScanner.scan_diff() and scan_file() both delegate to PatternEngine.scan()
File extension filtering is pre-match; AST approach must preserve this
No existing parser/AST infrastructure in codebase
test_coverage: Comprehensive unit tests in patterns.rs (test_pattern_engine_loads, test_hardcoded_api_key_detection, etc.) and scanner.rs (test_scan_diff_basic, test_scan_diff_line_numbers, test_scan_file_filters_ignored_paths). Integration tests in tests/integration_security.rs. Test fixture test_multi_line_vulnerability_not_detected documents the limitation. Coverage includes diff parsing, line tracking, ignore rules, and pattern matching.

library_findings.relevant_api: tree_sitter::Parser, tree_sitter::Language, tree_sitter::Query, tree_sitter::QueryCursor for AST parsing and querying
library_findings.notes: Requires tree-sitter language bindings (tree-sitter-rust, tree-sitter-python, etc.) for each language. Parser is reusable; queries are compiled once. Supports S-expression syntax for pattern matching across AST nodes.

library_findings.relevant_api: Regex::new(), Regex::find(), Regex::captures() for current line-by-line matching
library_findings.notes: Currently used for all 14 patterns; would be retained for simple single-line patterns in hybrid approach
Used to load patterns.json; PatternDefinition struct uses serde derive

approaches.name: Hybrid Regex+Tree-Sitter with Gradual Migration
approaches.description: Extend PatternDefinition to support both regex and tree-sitter query patterns. Add optional ast_query field. Modify PatternEngine to detect pattern type and route to appropriate scanner (RegexScanner vs TreeSitterScanner). Convert complex patterns (SQL injection, command injection) to tree-sitter queries first; keep simple patterns (hardcoded secrets) as regex. Implement per-language tree-sitter parser caching. Update Finding struct to support line ranges (start_line, end_line) for multi-line matches.
pros: Backward compatible: existing regex patterns continue to work
Allows incremental migration: convert patterns one at a time
Retains performance for simple patterns (regex is faster for single-line)
Reduces false positives by understanding syntax context
Enables detection of sophisticated multi-line vulnerabilities
cons: Increases code complexity: two scanning paths to maintain
Requires tree-sitter bindings for each language (Rust, Python, JS, etc.)
Tree-sitter parsers have larger binary footprint
Pattern definition format becomes more complex (union type)
Need to maintain both regex and query versions during transition

approaches.name: Pure Tree-Sitter Replacement with Language-Specific Queries
approaches.description: Replace PatternEngine.scan() entirely with tree-sitter-based scanning. Convert all 14 patterns to tree-sitter S-expression queries. Load language-specific parsers based on file extension. Compile queries once per language at startup. Store queries in patterns.json alongside metadata. Implement QueryCursor to find all matches in AST. Update Finding to include matched_range (start_line, end_line, start_col, end_col).
pros: Detects all multi-line vulnerabilities by design
Better understanding of code context reduces false positives
Enables sophisticated pattern matching (e.g., taint tracking across function calls)
Query syntax is more expressive than regex for code patterns
cons: Breaking change: all patterns must be rewritten as queries
Requires tree-sitter language bindings for supported languages
Larger binary size (tree-sitter parsers are substantial)
Slower startup (parsing entire file into AST)
Requires expertise in tree-sitter query syntax and each language's grammar

approaches.name: Lightweight Multi-Line Regex with Context Windows
approaches.description: Extend regex patterns to support multi-line matching by preprocessing code into context windows. Before scanning, split file into overlapping chunks (e.g., 5-line windows with 2-line overlap). Apply existing regex patterns to each window. Track original line numbers from context. Add new PatternDefinition.multiline_enabled flag. Keep PatternEngine.scan() mostly unchanged; add scan_multiline() method. No new dependencies required.
pros: Backward compatible: existing patterns work unchanged
Detects many multi-line vulnerabilities without full AST understanding
cons: Still regex-based: cannot detect true semantic vulnerabilities
Context window size is arbitrary; may miss patterns spanning >5 lines
False positives from pattern matches split across window boundaries
Does not scale well to complex vulnerability patterns

recommendation: Approach 1 (Hybrid Regex+Tree-Sitter with Gradual Migration) is recommended. It balances the need for multi-line detection with pragmatic constraints: (1) Backward compatibility allows shipping value incrementally without breaking existing patterns. (2) Gradual conversion lets the team learn tree-sitter idioms and validate query effectiveness before full commitment. (3) Retaining regex for simple patterns (hardcoded secrets) keeps performance and binary size reasonable. (4) The issue itself mentions 'hybrid approach' as the intended direction. Start by converting SQL injection and command injection patterns to tree-sitter queries (highest impact for multi-line detection), validate against test fixtures, then expand to other patterns. Implement per-language parser caching to mitigate startup cost. Update Finding struct to support line ranges early to unblock downstream consumers.
